skill.name: control-loop-extraction
skill.description: Extract and analyze agent reasoning loops, step functions, decision flow, and termination conditions in agent framework source code.
skill.summary: Documents how agent control loops reason, act, parse results, and stop.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown examples and prose in SKILL.md. No executable files, shell commands, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False positive external command detections in Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are fenced Python examples and inline control-flow examples that teach agent loop analysis. They are not executable skill scripts and do not invoke a shell or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown documentation, and the flagged code is inside examples or templates. No executable Ruby, shell, or script file exists in the scanned file structure.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography detections in prose
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain words such as description, Description, and design-related text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing implementation, or security-sensitive encryption logic is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review shows the lines are descriptive text and an output template. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, RC4, or other weak cryptography usage.

low_findings.title: False positive system reconnaissance detection in termination table
low_findings.description: The flagged line describes a step-limit termination condition for agent loops. It does not query host information, enumerate files, inspect processes, or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a Markdown table about loop termination behavior. No system reconnaissance command or data collection pattern is present.

content.value_statement: Agent frameworks often hide the code path that decides each reasoning step. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to identify loop patterns, step functions, and stop conditions.
actual_capabilities: Identifies ReAct, Plan-and-Solve, Reflection, and Tree-of-Thoughts loop patterns.
Locates the main agent execution loop in framework source code.
Documents input assembly, model invocation, output parsing, and action dispatch.
Catalogs termination conditions such as step limits, timeouts, finish actions, and error thresholds.
Produces a structured Markdown analysis for comparing agent frameworks.
limitations: Requires readable source code for the target agent framework.
Does not execute, benchmark, or modify the analyzed framework.
May need a prior codebase map for large or unfamiliar repositories.
Cannot prove runtime behavior when key logic is generated dynamically.

use_cases.title: Audit an Agent Framework
use_cases.description: Trace the reasoning loop and termination safeguards before adopting a framework.

use_cases.title: Document Internal Agent Logic
use_cases.description: Create a clear explanation of how an internal agent builds prompts, calls models, and dispatches tools.

use_cases.title: Compare Agent Architectures
use_cases.description: Normalize loop structure across several frameworks for architecture review or migration planning.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Main Loop
prompt_templates.prompt: Use control-loop-extraction to find the main agent execution loop in this repository. Report the file, function, and basic loop pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Map Step Function Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use control-loop-extraction to document the step function. Include input assembly, model call, response parsing, and dispatch behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Catalog Termination Conditions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use control-loop-extraction to list every loop exit condition. Include step limits, token limits, timeouts, finish actions, errors, and loop detection.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple Frameworks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use control-loop-extraction on these agent frameworks and compare their reasoning topology, step function design, dispatch model, and termination safeguards.

output_examples.output: The result identifies a Thought, Action, Observation cycle, names the loop location, and explains how observations update the next prompt.

output_examples.input: Review an agent with retries and tool dispatch.
output_examples.output: The result separates prompt construction, model invocation, parser behavior, tool routing, retry handling, and final-answer routing.
The result summarizes which agent plans once, which replans during execution, and which termination guards each implementation uses.

best_practices: Start with a codebase map so the analysis focuses on likely agent files.
Record exact file and line references for every loop, step function, and stop condition.
Separate observed code behavior from inferred architecture notes.
anti_patterns: Do not assume a framework uses ReAct because it has tool calls.
Do not ignore termination logic when the step function looks clear.
Do not report template examples as runtime behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill run the target agent framework?
faq.answer: No. It guides static analysis of source code and documentation.

faq.question: Which agent patterns can it identify?
faq.answer: It covers ReAct, Plan-and-Solve, Reflection, Tree-of-Thoughts, and hybrid patterns.

faq.question: Can it analyze large repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but it works best after a codebase mapping step identifies likely agent files.

faq.question: Does it generate architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: No. It produces structured written analysis that can feed separate diagram or comparison work.

faq.question: Can it find missing termination guards?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks the assistant to catalog stop conditions and flag loops without clear safeguards.

faq.question: Is it useful for security review?
faq.answer: It can support review by exposing tool dispatch and stop conditions, but it is not a full security scanner.
